wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Smart Voice Report project overview section. Explain the platform's purpose as an intelligent voice-enabled complaint management system for municipal governance. Document the core value proposition, target audience (citizens, ward administrators, super administrators), and key differentiators including AI-powered analytics and voice recognition. Describe the overall architecture combining React frontend with Node.js/Express backend, MongoDB database, and Supabase AI functions. Include the technology stack overview, deployment options, and integration capabilities. Provide context on the digital transformation impact for municipal services and citizen engagement.

wiki_catalogs.name: JWT Authentication Flow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the JWT authentication flow implementation. Detail the token generation process, payload structure including user ID, role, and ward information. Explain the token signing with JWT_SECRET, expiration handling, and secure storage in frontend applications. Document the authentication middleware that validates tokens, extracts user information, and attaches user context to requests. Cover token refresh strategies, logout mechanisms, and security considerations. Include practical examples of token usage in API requests, frontend authentication state management, and error handling for expired or invalid tokens.

wiki_catalogs.name: Voice Input and Recording
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the voice input and recording system. Detail the SpeechRecognition API implementation, microphone access handling, and audio context management. Explain the state management for different recording states (idle, listening, processing, error), error handling for microphone permissions and browser compatibility, and the audio analyser setup for waveform visualization. Document the component props including onTranscript and onPartialTranscript callbacks, buttonOnly mode for compact usage, and size variations. Include implementation examples showing proper microphone permission handling, fallback mechanisms for unsupported browsers, and cleanup procedures for audio resources. Address accessibility considerations and user experience patterns for voice input activation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Complaint Submission and Creation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the complaint submission and creation workflow. Detail the complete complaint creation process including voice-enabled and text-based submission methods. Document the strict ward validation requirements, complaint ID generation algorithm, and data validation rules. Explain the integration between voice input components and backend processing, including audio preprocessing and transcription. Cover the complaint data model structure, field definitions, and business logic for complaint creation. Include API endpoints for complaint submission, frontend components for voice and text input, and the notification system triggers for new complaints. Provide examples of complaint creation requests, validation scenarios, and error handling patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Sentiment Analysis Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the sentiment analysis engine covering real-time emotion detection and text analysis algorithms. Detail the custom NLP implementation with word scoring systems, emotional keyword detection, and contextual analysis. Document the sentiment scoring methodology, confidence thresholds, and classification accuracy metrics. Explain the integration with complaint text processing, real-time analysis workflows, and performance optimization techniques. Include implementation examples showing text preprocessing, sentiment calculation, and result interpretation. Cover the frontend sentiment visualization components and user interaction patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Basic Analytics Dashboard
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the basic analytics dashboard system. Document the real-time statistics display including complaint trends over time, ward performance rankings, resolution time analytics, and category correlation analysis. Explain the MongoDB aggregation pipeline implementation for data processing and the frontend visualization components. Detail the trend chart generation, resolution time calculations, and ward ranking algorithms. Include API endpoints for data retrieval, response schemas, and integration patterns. Cover the statistical calculations for performance metrics and the data visualization components used for trend representation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Achievement & Badge System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the achievement and badge system component. Document the comprehensive badge definition structure including milestone badges (first_report, active_citizen), engagement badges (popular_voice, supporter), impact badges (problem_solver, community_hero), and streak-based badges (streak_starter, week_warrior). Explain the dynamic badge calculation system that queries actual grievance data rather than stored counters. Detail the badge categories, requirements, and award mechanisms. Cover the badge progress tracking, unlock notifications, and visual display components. Include implementation examples for badge qualification checks, progress calculations, and user interface integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: PWA Service Worker & Offline Capabilities
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the PWA service worker implementation and offline functionality. Document the service worker registration process, lifecycle management, and update mechanisms. Explain offline caching strategies, cache-first vs network-first patterns, and data persistence techniques. Detail the background sync capabilities for offline complaint submissions and data synchronization. Cover the service worker event handlers, message passing between worker and app, and error handling strategies. Include configuration options, performance optimization techniques, and debugging approaches for service worker issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Super Admin Dashboard
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the super admin dashboard system. Detail the main administrative interface including the navigation sidebar, dashboard overview cards, analytics charts, and recent complaints table. Document the admin layout components, responsive design patterns, and interactive elements. Explain the data visualization components including bar charts for ward complaints, pie charts for issue type distribution, and line charts for resolution trends. Cover the real-time data fetching mechanisms, authentication integration, and admin-specific features like user management access and system configuration options.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core User & Complaint Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for the core User, Grievance, and Feedback entities. Detail the User model with authentication fields (email, password), profile information (name, phone), engagement metrics (points, complaintsSubmitted, upvotes), gamification tracking (badges, streaks, challenges), and notification preferences. Document the Grievance model with complaint details (description, category, location), status tracking (status, priority, assignedTo), AI intelligence fields (sentimentScore, categoryPrediction, duplicateDetection), and temporal fields (createdAt, updatedAt). Explain the Feedback model for user responses including rating, comment, and resolution feedback. Include field validations, data types, constraints, and relationship patterns between these core entities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication & User Management APIs
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for authentication and user management endpoints. Document user registration API with email/password validation, user profile creation, and role assignment. Detail login API with JWT token generation, session management, and multi-factor authentication integration. Cover logout functionality and token invalidation. Document 2FA setup and verification APIs including QR code generation, backup codes, and authentication flow. Include authentication middleware implementation, token validation, and role-based access control. Provide request/response schemas, error codes, security considerations, and client implementation examples.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication & User State
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the authentication state management system. Explain the AuthProvider context implementation, user session handling, and multi-role authentication (citizen, ward admin, super admin). Document the authentication flow including login, signup, logout, and 2FA integration. Cover state persistence using localStorage, storage event listeners for cross-tab synchronization, and loading states during initialization. Include practical examples of protected route implementation, role-based rendering, and authentication guards. Address error handling, token management, and security considerations for state updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Security & Access Control
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive authentication security documentation covering JWT token implementation, multi-role access control, and session management. Detail the authenticateUser middleware that validates tokens across Admin, WardAdmin, and User collections. Document the authorizeRoles middleware for role-based authorization and authorizeWardAccess for ward-specific data filtering. Explain the two-factor authentication security measures, token expiration handling, and account status validation. Include security best practices for JWT secret management, token refresh strategies, and secure logout procedures. Address common authentication vulnerabilities and their prevention.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Library & Design System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the component library and design system integration. Detail the shadcn/ui component library setup, customization patterns, and utility functions. Explain the design system architecture including color schemes, typography, spacing, and component variants. Document the component composition patterns, prop interfaces, and styling strategies using Tailwind CSS. Cover the utility functions for class merging, conditional styling, and responsive design. Include component customization guidelines, theme integration, and accessibility compliance. Provide examples of extending the design system and creating custom component variants.

wiki_catalogs.name: Automated Testing Framework
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the automated testing framework used in the Smart Voice Report platform. Detail the validation suite architecture, test execution流程, and testing methodologies. Document the core validation script (validation-suite.js) including its five-category testing approach covering backend health checks, analytics API validation, 2FA authentication flow testing, frontend availability checks, and database connectivity verification. Explain the test request utilities, HTTP client implementation, and error handling strategies. Cover the phase 2 validation suite for advanced testing scenarios. Include setup instructions, test data management, and continuous integration practices. Provide examples of extending the testing framework and adding new test cases.

wiki_catalogs.name: Build & Packaging Process
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the build and packaging processes covering frontend and backend compilation. Document the Vite build configuration including development and production settings, asset optimization, and cache busting strategies. Explain the React application bundling process, dependency optimization, and static asset handling. Detail the backend Node.js build process, TypeScript compilation, and package management. Include environment-specific configurations, build scripts, and deployment artifacts preparation. Cover performance optimization techniques, bundle analysis, and troubleshooting common build issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for the Smart Voice Report platform. Include detailed prerequisites (Node.js, npm, MongoDB), step-by-step installation instructions for both frontend and backend components, environment configuration setup, database initialization, and local development server startup. Document the project structure, key configuration files, and essential environment variables. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common setup issues, dependency conflicts, and database connection problems. Include verification steps to ensure proper installation and initial testing procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Multi-Factor Authentication (2FA)
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the multi-factor authentication system. Explain the 2FA requirement for citizen users, setup process for enabling 2FA, and verification workflows. Document the TOTP (Time-based One-Time Password) implementation, QR code generation, and backup code creation. Cover the temporary setup tokens for initial 2FA configuration, verification token validation, and security measures against brute force attacks. Include frontend components for 2FA setup and verification, error handling for invalid codes, and user experience considerations. Address 2FA recovery scenarios and administrative controls.

wiki_catalogs.name: Speech Processing and Recognition
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the speech processing and recognition pipeline. Explain the Web Speech API integration, continuous recognition setup, and interim vs final transcript handling. Document the speech recognition configuration including language settings, continuous vs single sentence modes, and result processing algorithms. Cover the audio analysis implementation using Web Audio API, frequency domain analysis, and real-time waveform generation. Detail the error handling strategies for various speech recognition errors (network, not allowed, no speech), timeout handling, and automatic cleanup procedures. Include examples of processing partial transcripts, handling recognition interruptions, and implementing retry mechanisms. Address browser compatibility considerations and feature detection patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Complaint Status Tracking and Updates
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for complaint status tracking and update mechanisms. Explain the complaint lifecycle from submission to resolution, including status transitions and their business implications. Document the role-based access control for status updates, particularly the ward-based restrictions for ward administrators. Cover the audit logging system for status changes, including who made changes and when. Detail the notification system for status updates, including email and push notifications. Explain the public complaint tracking interface and how citizens can monitor their complaint status. Include examples of status update requests, access control scenarios, and notification triggers for different status changes.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Complaint Analyzer
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the AI complaint analyzer covering machine learning-based complaint processing, categorization, and intelligent routing. Document the complaint analysis pipeline including text preprocessing, feature extraction, and classification algorithms. Explain the category suggestion system with confidence scoring, priority prediction models, and duplicate detection mechanisms. Detail the smart routing recommendations based on department capacity and historical data. Cover the batch processing capabilities, performance optimization, and accuracy metrics. Include practical examples of complaint analysis workflows, integration patterns with the frontend, and administrative dashboard features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Geographic Analytics & Heatmaps
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the geographic analytics and heatmap system. Document the geographic heatmap generation algorithm, SLA monitoring implementation, and spatial data visualization. Explain the heatmap service architecture, coordinate processing, and intensity mapping for complaint density. Detail the SLA alert panel functionality for monitoring service level agreements across different wards. Cover the geospatial data processing, heat intensity calculations, and real-time update mechanisms. Include the integration with mapping libraries, data formatting for visualization, and performance optimization for large datasets.

wiki_catalogs.name: Leaderboard & Ranking System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the leaderboard and ranking system. Detail the user scoring algorithm that calculates points based on complaints submitted (10 pts), upvotes received (5 pts), and complaints resolved (15 pts). Document the ranking calculation methodology, position updates, and leaderboard refresh mechanisms. Explain the rank change animations and visual indicators for user progress. Cover the leaderboard data structure, sorting algorithms, and performance optimization for large user bases. Include examples of score calculations, rank positioning, and leaderboard display components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Mobile Optimization & Responsive Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for mobile optimization and responsive design implementation. Document the mobile detection hooks, responsive breakpoints, and adaptive UI components. Explain touch-friendly interface design patterns, gesture handling, and mobile-specific interactions. Cover viewport configuration, meta tags for mobile optimization, and performance considerations for mobile devices. Detail the responsive component patterns, mobile navigation strategies, and device-specific feature detection. Include testing methodologies for cross-platform compatibility and optimization techniques for different screen sizes and orientations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Ward Administrator Interface
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the ward administrator interface system. Document the ward-specific administrative layout, dashboard components showing ward-level analytics, complaint management interface, and ward information management. Explain the ward admin role permissions, ward-specific data filtering, and department assignment workflows. Cover the complaint review processes, status update mechanisms, and performance monitoring features. Include the ward information management system for updating ward details and managing ward-specific configurations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Administrative Models & Permissions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed data model documentation for administrative entities covering Admin and WardAdmin models with role-specific attributes and access permissions. Document the Admin model with superuser privileges, system-wide access controls, and administrative capabilities. Explain the WardAdmin model with ward-specific permissions, department assignments, and localized administrative functions. Detail the WardAdminInvitation model for managing admin invitations and verification processes. Include role hierarchies, permission matrices, access control patterns, and security considerations. Document the separation of citizen users from administrative users and the multi-tier authentication system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Complaint Management APIs
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for complaint management endpoints. Document complaint submission API with voice transcription, text processing, and AI categorization integration. Detail complaint tracking API for status updates, department assignment, and priority management. Cover complaint listing APIs for citizen view, admin review, and ward-level filtering. Document complaint update endpoints for status changes, resolution updates, and user feedback. Include request/response schemas for complaint creation, status transitions, and search functionality. Provide examples of complaint workflows, error handling for invalid submissions, and integration patterns with voice recognition services.

wiki_catalogs.name: Notification & Preference State
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for notification and user preference state management. Detail the notification context provider for managing toast notifications, push notification subscriptions, and user preference settings. Document the toast hook implementation for displaying alerts, success messages, and error notifications. Cover notification sound preferences, push notification permissions, and subscription management. Include examples of notification triggers, custom toast configurations, and preference persistence. Address notification delivery mechanisms, user consent handling, and cross-device synchronization of preferences.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Protection & Privacy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed data protection and privacy documentation covering data encryption, input validation, and sensitive data handling. Document password hashing implementation, data sanitization techniques, and protection against SQL injection and XSS attacks. Explain secure email and SMS transmission, data retention policies, and user consent management. Cover error handling security practices, audit logging for data access, and secure deletion procedures. Include guidelines for protecting citizen personal information, complaint data privacy, and administrative access logs.

wiki_catalogs.name: Page Components & Layout System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for page components and layout system architecture. Explain the routing structure using React Router, protected routes implementation, and layout composition patterns. Document the main application layout with conditional navigation and footer rendering. Detail the administrative layouts for different user roles including super admin and ward admin interfaces. Cover the page component hierarchy, route guards, and authentication integration. Include responsive design patterns, layout switching logic, and mobile-first approach. Provide examples of creating new page components, implementing route protection, and managing layout variations for different user types.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Integration & Authentication Testing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for API integration testing and authentication validation processes. Document the API integration test suite covering endpoint validation, request/response testing, and service integration verification. Explain the authentication testing framework including login validation, session management testing, and role-based access control verification. Detail the 2FA authentication testing procedures, validation workflows, and security testing protocols. Include the 2FA validation report analysis, authentication flow testing, and multi-factor authentication validation processes. Cover test data preparation, mock service integration, and testing against different user roles. Provide debugging strategies for authentication failures, API endpoint issues, and integration problems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Infrastructure & Environment Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive infrastructure and environment setup documentation covering database configuration, AI function deployment, and environment variable management. Detail MongoDB connection setup, database migration procedures, and connection pooling configuration. Document Supabase AI function deployment including TypeScript functions for AI categorization, chatbot, and email notifications. Explain environment variable structure, secrets management, and configuration validation. Include infrastructure requirements, scaling considerations, and resource allocation guidelines. Cover network configuration, firewall settings, and security group setup for production environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication & Authorization System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive authentication and authorization documentation for the multi-role system. Detail the JWT-based authentication flow, role hierarchy (citizen, ward admin, super admin), and 2FA implementation. Document the authentication middleware, token validation processes, and session management. Explain the user registration workflow, password hashing, and security measures. Cover the two-factor authentication setup, verification processes, and backup codes. Include API endpoints for authentication, protected route implementation, and role-based access control patterns. Provide examples of authentication state management in the frontend and security best practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the role-based access control system. Detail the three-tier role hierarchy (user/citizen, ward_admin, admin) and their respective permissions. Explain the role validation during login, middleware enforcement of access restrictions, and route protection mechanisms. Document the user registration process, automatic role assignment, and role upgrade/downgrade procedures. Cover the protected route implementation, frontend role checking, and dynamic UI rendering based on user roles. Include examples of role-specific API access, administrative privileges, and security boundaries between different user types.

wiki_catalogs.name: Voice Navigation and Control
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the voice navigation and control system. Explain the voice command interpretation for application navigation, form control manipulation, and user interface interactions. Document the voice command parsing algorithms, intent recognition for navigation actions, and integration with the application routing system. Cover the voice form control functionality for hands-free form filling, voice-driven UI interactions, and accessibility features for users with mobility impairments. Detail the command vocabulary, context-aware responses, and error recovery mechanisms. Include implementation examples showing voice command registration, custom command mapping, and integration with existing navigation components. Address voice recognition accuracy improvements and user training patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Department Assignment and Routing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the department assignment and smart routing system. Explain the complaint categorization process and how complaints are automatically routed to appropriate departments based on category and location. Document the smart routing algorithms, including geographic proximity and department workload balancing. Cover the manual assignment capabilities for administrators and the criteria used for optimal routing. Detail the integration with the geographic heatmap service and SLA monitoring for routing efficiency. Explain the priority-based routing system and how high-priority complaints receive expedited processing. Include examples of routing scenarios, assignment rules, and performance metrics for routing effectiveness.

wiki_catalogs.name: Predictive Analytics System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the predictive analytics system covering trend forecasting, pattern recognition, and future issue prediction algorithms. Detail the time series analysis implementation, seasonal pattern detection, and anomaly identification methods. Document the machine learning models used for predictive modeling, feature engineering processes, and accuracy validation techniques. Explain the integration with complaint data streams, real-time prediction updates, and dashboard visualization components. Cover the statistical analysis methodologies, confidence intervals, and prediction reliability metrics. Include implementation examples showing forecast generation, trend analysis workflows, and administrative reporting features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Custom Dashboard Builder
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the custom dashboard builder and advanced analytics components. Document the dashboard creation interface, widget management system, and custom visualization tools. Explain the dashboard renderer architecture, widget editor functionality, and historical comparison features. Detail the pattern insights generation, performance benchmarking, and widget customization options. Cover the drag-and-drop interface implementation, widget persistence mechanisms, and export functionality. Include the advanced analytics features like historical data comparison and pattern recognition algorithms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Streak & Activity Tracking
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the streak and activity tracking system. Document the streak calculation logic including current streak maintenance, longest streak tracking, and streak reset conditions. Explain the daily activity detection mechanism and streak preservation strategies. Detail the streak-based badge system (streak_starter, week_warrior, dedicated_citizen) and their requirements. Cover the streak widget UI components, streak visualization, and user motivation features. Include implementation examples for streak updates, activity logging, and streak persistence mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: PWA Installation & Notification System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for PWA installation prompts and notification system. Document the BeforeInstallPrompt event handling, installation eligibility checks, and user choice management. Explain the offline indicator component, connection status detection, and user feedback mechanisms. Cover notification permissions, push notification setup, and cross-browser compatibility. Detail the installation prompt UI components, user experience considerations, and fallback strategies for browsers that don't support PWA installation. Include testing approaches for installation flows and troubleshooting common installation issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Administrative Workflows & Reporting
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for administrative workflows and reporting systems. Detail the complaint handling workflows including review processes, status assignments, and resolution tracking. Document the feedback management system for processing citizen feedback and suggestions. Explain the advanced analytics reporting interface with custom dashboard creation, data export capabilities, and performance metrics. Cover the administrative reporting features including compliance reports, audit trails, and system usage analytics. Include workflow automation features, batch processing capabilities, and administrative approval processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Supporting Models & Relationships
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for supporting data models including Challenge, AuditLog, SMSLog, and CustomDashboard entities. Detail the Challenge model with gamification parameters (daily/weekly/monthly challenges), participation tracking, and reward systems. Document the AuditLog model for system activity tracking, user actions logging, and compliance purposes. Explain the SMSLog model for SMS delivery tracking, message history, and communication analytics. Cover the CustomDashboard model for user-configurable analytics displays, widget configurations, and personalization settings. Include relationship patterns with core models, indexing strategies for performance optimization, and integration points with the broader system architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: Analytics & Reporting APIs
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for analytics and reporting endpoints. Document basic analytics APIs for complaint statistics, trend analysis, and performance metrics. Detail advanced analytics APIs for custom dashboard creation, widget management, and data visualization. Cover predictive analytics endpoints for trend forecasting, issue prediction, and capacity planning. Include export functionality APIs for CSV, PDF, and data download. Document geographic analytics endpoints for heatmaps, SLA monitoring, and ward performance comparison. Provide request/response schemas, data filtering options, pagination support, and real-time data streaming capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Custom Hooks & Utilities
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for custom React hooks and utility functions. Document the mobile detection hook for responsive design adaptation, toast notification utility for consistent messaging, and feedback collection hooks. Cover mobile optimization utilities for PWA features, notification preference management, and user experience enhancements. Include practical examples of hook usage in components, performance optimization techniques, and integration patterns. Address hook dependencies, cleanup procedures, and best practices for custom hook development. Document utility functions for data fetching, state management, and UI enhancement features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Compliance & Regulatory Requirements
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create compliance and regulatory requirements documentation covering GDPR adherence, data protection regulations, and legal obligations. Detail user data rights management, consent mechanisms, and data subject requests handling. Document data retention schedules, secure data deletion procedures, and breach notification protocols. Explain international data transfer restrictions, cookie compliance, and electronic evidence preservation for complaints. Include policy documentation, user agreements, and regulatory reporting requirements for municipal governance data handling.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI Components & Styling
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for individual UI components and styling implementation. Document each UI component including buttons, forms, modals, navigation elements, and interactive components. Explain the component props, events, and customization options. Detail the styling approach using Tailwind CSS classes, responsive design patterns, and dark/light theme support. Cover component accessibility features, keyboard navigation, and screen reader compatibility. Include component composition patterns, state management integration, and animation implementations. Provide usage examples, best practices for component reuse, and guidelines for maintaining consistent styling across the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Validation Reports & Quality Metrics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for validation reports and quality metrics collection systems. Detail the implementation validation reports including feature completeness assessment, performance benchmarking, and quality assurance metrics. Document the phase 2 implementation summary covering advanced feature validation, enhancement testing, and system improvement verification. Explain the notification enhancement plan validation, migration summary reports, and technical debt assessment. Cover quality metrics collection, performance monitoring, and continuous improvement tracking. Include report generation processes, data analysis methodologies, and stakeholder reporting formats. Provide guidance on interpreting validation results, identifying improvement areas, and maintaining quality standards throughout the development lifecycle.

wiki_catalogs.name: Monitoring & Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed monitoring and operations documentation covering system observability, performance monitoring, and operational procedures. Document error tracking and logging configuration, performance metrics collection, and health check endpoints. Explain monitoring dashboard setup, alerting mechanisms, and incident response procedures. Detail operational tasks including log rotation, backup procedures, and system maintenance. Include troubleshooting guides for common operational issues, performance optimization strategies, and capacity planning. Cover security monitoring, audit logging, and compliance reporting requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Voice-Enabled Complaint System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the voice-enabled complaint submission system. Explain the speech recognition implementation, audio processing pipeline, and waveform visualization components. Document the voice input controls, recording functionality, and real-time audio feedback. Cover the voice navigation system for hands-free operation, voice search capabilities, and voice response generation. Detail the browser API integration, microphone permissions, audio quality optimization, and fallback mechanisms. Include implementation examples, error handling for voice recognition failures, and accessibility considerations for users with disabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Session Management & Security
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for session management and security implementation. Explain password hashing with bcrypt, password validation rules, and security constraints preventing weak passwords. Document session timeout handling, token expiration policies, and automatic logout mechanisms. Cover security measures including CSRF protection, rate limiting, and account lockout policies. Detail the password reset workflow, email verification processes, and security audit logging. Include frontend session persistence, automatic token refresh, and secure cookie handling. Address common security vulnerabilities and mitigation strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Voice Response and Interaction
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the voice response and interaction system. Detail the text-to-speech implementation, synthesized voice generation, and audio playback management. Explain the voice search functionality including query processing, result presentation, and interactive search experiences. Document the response generation algorithms, natural language processing for conversational interfaces, and contextual response handling. Cover the audio output management, speech rate and pitch control, and pronunciation optimization. Include implementation examples showing voice feedback integration, search result narration, and interactive voice assistance. Address accessibility features for visually impaired users, voice quality optimization, and integration with screen readers and assistive technologies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Complaint Analytics and Reporting
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for complaint analytics and reporting capabilities. Detail the administrative dashboard statistics including total complaints, pending vs resolved ratios, and ward performance metrics. Explain the ward-wise complaint distribution analysis and issue type categorization reporting. Document the resolution trend analysis showing monthly resolution patterns and SLA compliance tracking. Cover the public transparency features including searchable complaint databases and anonymized reporting. Detail the integration with geographic heatmaps for visualizing complaint hotspots and the ward ranking system for performance comparison. Include examples of analytics queries, dashboard configurations, and export capabilities for data sharing.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Model Integration & Supabase Functions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the AI model integration architecture covering Supabase Edge Functions, model deployment strategies, and external AI service connectivity. Explain the database schema modifications for AI metadata storage, model versioning, and performance tracking. Include deployment configurations, environment setup, and monitoring approaches for AI-powered features.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the predictive analytics and visualization system. Explain the public transparency panel for citizen-facing analytics, export functionality for data sharing, and real-time prediction updates. Detail the machine learning models used for forecasting, confidence interval calculations, and prediction accuracy metrics. Cover the visualization components for presenting predictive data, export formats (CSV, PDF, Excel), and data sharing protocols. Include the integration with external analytics tools and performance optimization for large-scale predictions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Challenge & Motivation System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the challenge and motivation system. Detail the challenge creation and management functionality including challenge types, difficulty levels, and completion criteria. Document the challenge board interface for displaying active challenges, user participation tracking, and reward distribution. Explain the motivation algorithms that encourage user engagement through goal-setting and achievement tracking. Cover challenge persistence, user progress monitoring, and integration with the broader gamification framework. Include examples of challenge implementation, user interaction patterns, and success metrics.

wiki_catalogs.name: Administrative APIs
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for administrative management endpoints. Document super admin APIs for user management, system configuration, and complaint oversight. Detail ward administrator APIs for ward-specific complaint handling, performance monitoring, and staff management. Cover user management endpoints for profile updates, role assignments, and access control. Document feedback management APIs for citizen feedback collection, analysis, and response tracking. Include administrative dashboard endpoints for system metrics, user analytics, and complaint trends. Provide request/response schemas, permission requirements, audit logging, and administrative workflow examples.

wiki_catalogs.name: Complaint Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive complaint management documentation covering the complete lifecycle from submission to resolution. Detail the complaint creation workflow, status tracking mechanisms, department assignment processes, and priority management systems. Document the complaint data model, field definitions, validation rules, and business logic. Explain the integration between voice and text complaint submission, duplicate detection algorithms, and complaint categorization. Cover the administrative workflows for complaint review, assignment, and resolution tracking. Include API endpoints for complaint operations, frontend components for complaint management, and reporting capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: AI Intelligence & Analytics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive AI intelligence and analytics documentation covering the machine learning and data analysis capabilities. Detail the sentiment analysis engine, custom NLP processing, and real-time emotion detection algorithms. Document the AI complaint analyzer with category classification, priority assignment, and duplicate detection. Explain the predictive analytics system for trend forecasting and future issue prediction. Cover the integration of Supabase AI functions, model training processes, and performance optimization. Include implementation examples, data preprocessing requirements, and accuracy metrics for AI-powered features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Advanced Analytics & Dashboard System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed analytics and dashboard documentation covering the comprehensive reporting and visualization system. Document the basic analytics dashboard with real-time statistics, complaint trends, and performance metrics. Explain the advanced analytics features including geographic heatmaps, SLA monitoring, and custom dashboard builder. Detail the dashboard widget system, export functionality, and data visualization components. Cover the geographic analytics implementation, correlation analysis, and trend chart generation. Include the predictive analytics dashboard for future issue forecasting and the public transparency panel for citizen access.

wiki_catalogs.name: Gamification & User Engagement System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive gamification and engagement system documentation covering the achievement and motivation platforms. Detail the badge creation and management system, achievement criteria, and reward distribution mechanisms. Document the leaderboard system with ranking algorithms, score calculation, and competition management. Explain the streak tracking service for activity consistency, challenge board for goal setting, and motivation algorithms. Cover the integration between gamification features and user engagement metrics. Include implementation examples for badge unlocking, leaderboard updates, and streak maintenance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Mobile Experience & PWA Features
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed mobile experience and PWA documentation covering the progressive web app implementation. Document the service worker configuration, offline functionality, and app installation prompts. Explain the mobile optimization strategies, responsive design patterns, and touch-friendly interfaces. Detail the push notification system, background sync capabilities, and offline data persistence. Cover the mobile-specific components including offline indicators, PWA install prompts, and mobile navigation patterns. Include performance optimization techniques for mobile devices and testing strategies for cross-platform compatibility.

wiki_catalogs.name: Administrative Interfaces
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive administrative interface documentation covering the multi-tier admin system. Detail the super admin dashboard with system oversight, user management, and analytics monitoring. Document the ward administrator interface for ward-specific management, complaint handling, and performance monitoring. Explain the admin layout components, navigation patterns, and role-specific features. Cover the administrative workflows for user administration, complaint review, and system configuration. Include the reporting interfaces, feedback management, and administrative analytics dashboards.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Models & Database Schema
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation covering the MongoDB schema design and entity relationships. Detail the User model with authentication fields, profile information, and engagement metrics. Document the Grievance model with complaint details, status tracking, and AI intelligence fields. Explain the Admin and WardAdmin models with role-specific attributes and access permissions. Cover the Feedback model for user responses, Challenge model for gamification, and supporting models for audit logs and SMS tracking. Include database relationships, indexing strategies, and query optimization patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation covering all RESTful endpoints and their specifications. Document authentication APIs including user registration, login, logout, and 2FA endpoints. Detail complaint management APIs for submission, tracking, updates, and status changes. Cover analytics APIs for data retrieval, export functionality, and real-time updates. Explain administrative APIs for user management, complaint oversight, and system configuration. Include request/response schemas, authentication requirements, error handling, rate limiting, and versioning information. Provide practical examples and client implementation guidelines.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Component Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed frontend component architecture documentation covering the React component system and design patterns. Document the shadcn/ui component library integration, custom component development, and styling strategies using Tailwind CSS. Explain the component composition patterns, prop interfaces, and state management integration. Detail the UI component system including buttons, dialogs, forms, and navigation elements. Cover the page component architecture, layout systems, and responsive design implementation. Include component reusability patterns, accessibility compliance, and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management & Context
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive state management documentation covering the React context providers and custom hooks. Detail the authentication state management, notification state handling, and theme switching functionality. Explain the complaints context for data fetching and state synchronization. Document the custom hooks for toast notifications, mobile optimization, and user preferences. Cover the integration between context providers, React hooks, and component state. Include state persistence strategies, error boundary implementation, and performance optimization for state updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security & Compliance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive security and compliance documentation covering authentication security, data protection, and regulatory adherence. Detail the JWT token security, password hashing implementation, and session management. Document the two-factor authentication security measures, email and SMS verification processes. Explain the input validation, SQL injection prevention, and XSS protection strategies. Cover GDPR compliance requirements, data retention policies, and user consent management. Include security audit logging, error handling security, and secure deployment practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing & Quality Assurance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive testing and quality assurance documentation covering the validation framework and testing strategies. Detail the automated testing suite including unit tests, integration tests, and end-to-end validation. Document the API integration testing, authentication testing, and 2FA validation processes. Explain the implementation validation reports, performance testing, and quality assurance workflows. Cover the testing environment setup, test data management, and continuous integration practices. Include debugging strategies, performance profiling, and quality metrics collection.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment & DevOps
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment and DevOps documentation covering the production deployment strategies and infrastructure setup. Detail the frontend build process, backend deployment configurations, and database migration procedures. Document the Supabase AI function deployment, environment variable management, and CI/CD pipeline setup. Explain the monitoring and logging configuration, performance optimization, and scaling strategies. Cover the infrastructure requirements, security hardening, and disaster recovery planning. Include deployment automation, rollback procedures, and operational maintenance tasks.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting & FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting and FAQ documentation addressing common issues and their solutions. Detail authentication problems, voice recognition issues, database connectivity problems, and performance optimization. Document error handling strategies, debugging techniques, and diagnostic procedures. Explain common deployment issues, environment configuration problems, and browser compatibility concerns. Cover troubleshooting tools, logging strategies, and monitoring approaches. Include frequently asked questions about system limitations, feature availability, and user guidance.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Smart Voice Report - Comprehensive Project Overview

## Project Introduction

**Smart Voice Report** is an innovative digital governance platform designed to modernize citizen-government interaction through intelligent voice-enabled complaint management and analytics. The system serves as a comprehensive solution for municipal governance, enabling citizens to report issues through voice commands while providing advanced analytics and AI-powered insights for city administrators.

### Core Goals and Objectives

- **Citizen Empowerment**: Provide accessible, voice-first complaint submission for diverse demographics
- **Administrative Efficiency**: Streamline grievance processing with AI-driven categorization and routing
- **Data-Driven Governance**: Enable evidence-based decision making through advanced analytics and reporting
- **Transparency**: Maintain public accountability through comprehensive audit trails and progress tracking

### Target Audience

- **Citizens**: Residents seeking convenient complaint submission methods
- **City Administrators**: Ward officials and department heads managing municipal services
- **System Administrators**: Platform maintainers overseeing the entire ecosystem

## Technical Architecture

The project follows a modern microservices architecture with clear separation between frontend, backend, and AI processing layers.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Frontend React App] --> B[Express.js Backend API]
    B --> C[MongoDB Database]
    B --> D[AI Processing Layer]
    D --> E[Supabase Functions]
    D --> F[External APIs]
    B --> G[Notification Services]
    G --> H[Email Service]
    G --> I[SMS Service]
    G --> J[Push Notifications]
```

### Component Breakdown

The architecture consists of three primary layers:

1. **Presentation Layer**: React-based frontend with modern UI components
2. **Application Layer**: Node.js/Express backend with RESTful APIs
3. **Data Layer**: MongoDB with comprehensive indexing and caching strategies

### Design Patterns

- **MVC Pattern**: Clear separation of concerns across models, views, and controllers
- **Repository Pattern**: Abstracted data access layer for database operations
- **Observer Pattern**: Real-time notifications and state management
- **Factory Pattern**: Dynamic component creation for UI elements

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Frontend Entry Point**
- `Frontend/src/main.jsx` - Application bootstrap and error handling
- `Frontend/src/App.jsx` - Routing configuration and provider setup

**Backend Entry Point**
- `backend/server.js` - Server initialization and database connection
- `backend/src/app.js` - API routing and middleware configuration

### Core Modules

**API Service Layer**
- `Frontend/src/services/apiService.js` - Centralized HTTP client with comprehensive endpoints
- `backend/src/controllers/` - Business logic controllers organized by domain
- `backend/src/routes/` - API endpoint definitions and parameter validation

**Data Models**
- `backend/src/models/Grievance.js` - Primary complaint tracking with AI intelligence fields
- `backend/src/models/User.js` - User management with role-based access control
- `backend/src/models/Admin.js` - Administrative user profiles

### Configuration Approach

**Environment Configuration**
- `backend/.env` - Database connection and service credentials
- `Frontend/.env` - API endpoints and feature flags
- `backend/src/config/db.js` - MongoDB connection management

**Build Configuration**
- `Frontend/vite.config.ts` - Development and production build settings
- `Frontend/tailwind.config.js` - Styling and component customization
- `backend/package.json` - Node.js dependencies and scripts

### External Dependencies

**Frontend Dependencies**
- React 18.3.1 with modern hooks and concurrent features
- Radix UI primitives for accessible component foundation
- Tailwind CSS for utility-first styling
- Framer Motion for smooth animations and transitions

**Backend Dependencies**
- Express.js 4.19.2 for RESTful API development
- Mongoose 8.5.2 for MongoDB object modeling
- JWT authentication for secure API access
- Nodemailer for email notifications

### Integration Points

**Voice Processing Integration**
- Web Speech API for browser-based voice recognition
- Real-time audio visualization with Web Audio API
- Voice command processing and natural language understanding

**AI Intelligence Integration**
- Custom AI categorization service for complaint classification
- Sentiment analysis for emotional tone detection
- Predictive analytics for trend forecasting

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant F as Frontend
    participant B as Backend
    participant DB as Database
    participant AI as AI Service
    
    U->>F: Voice Command
    F->>B: API Request
    B->>AI: AI Analysis
    AI->>B: Classification Result
    B->>DB: Store Complaint
    DB->>B: Confirmation
    B->>F: Response
    F->>U: Success Message
```

## Key Features

### Voice-Enabled Complaint Submission

The platform revolutionizes traditional complaint processes through intelligent voice interaction:

**Voice Input Component**
- `Frontend/src/components/VoiceInput.jsx` - Real-time speech recognition with waveform visualization
- Support for multiple languages and dialects
- Real-time transcription with confidence scoring
- Visual feedback during recording sessions

**Voice Navigation System**
- `Frontend/src/components/voice/VoiceNavigation.jsx` - Voice-controlled application navigation
- Context-aware command processing
- Progressive disclosure for complex workflows

### Advanced Analytics Dashboard

**Multi-Dimensional Analytics**
- `Frontend/src/components/analytics/` - Comprehensive analytical components
- Real-time trend visualization with Recharts integration
- Geographic heatmaps for spatial analysis
- Predictive modeling for future issue patterns

**Administrative Insights**
- `Frontend/src/pages/admin/` - Role-based administrative interfaces
- Performance benchmarking against city standards
- SLA compliance monitoring
- Public transparency reporting

### Intelligent Complaint Routing

**AI-Powered Categorization**
- `Frontend/src/components/ai/AIComplaintAnalyzer.jsx` - Machine learning-based complaint classification
- Priority assignment based on urgency and impact assessment
- Duplicate detection and merging capabilities
- Department assignment optimization

**Sentiment Analysis**
- `Frontend/src/components/ai/SentimentAnalyzer.jsx` - Emotional tone detection in complaints
- Public mood tracking for policy evaluation
- Early warning system for emerging issues

### Gamification and Engagement

**Citizen Recognition System**
- `Frontend/src/components/engagement/` - Interactive engagement components
- Achievement badges for active participation
- Leaderboard system for community competition
- Streak-based motivation for consistent reporting

**Mobile Experience Enhancement**
- `Frontend/src/components/mobile/` - Progressive Web App features
- Offline capability with service worker implementation
- Push notification system for updates
- Native app installation prompts

### Security and Compliance

**Multi-Factor Authentication**
- `backend/src/controllers/twoFactorAuthController.js` - Secure authentication with time-based codes
- QR code generation for authenticator apps
- Backup code generation for recovery scenarios
- Role-based access control enforcement

**Data Protection**
- End-to-end encryption for sensitive communications
- GDPR-compliant data handling and retention policies
- Comprehensive audit logging for all administrative actions
- Regular security vulnerability assessments

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[User Registration] --> B[Email Verification]
    B --> C[2FA Setup]
    C --> D[Role Assignment]
    D --> E[Access Granted]
    
    F[Complaint Submission] --> G[Voice Processing]
    G --> H[AI Analysis]
    H --> I[Category Assignment]
    I --> J[Priority Setting]
    J --> K[Department Routing]
    
    L[Administrative Action] --> M[Status Update]
    M --> N[Notification Trigger]
    N --> O[User Communication]
```

This comprehensive architecture enables Smart Voice Report to serve as a complete digital governance solution, combining cutting-edge voice technology with robust administrative tools to create an efficient, transparent, and citizen-centric municipal service platform.

**Sources:**
- [package.json](Frontend/package.json)
- [package.json](backend/package.json)
- [App.jsx](Frontend/src/App.jsx)
- [server.js](backend/server.js)
- [app.js](backend/src/app.js)
- [main.jsx](Frontend/src/main.jsx)
- [db.js](backend/src/config/db.js)
- [apiService.js](Frontend/src/services/apiService.js)
- [auth-context.jsx](Frontend/src/context/auth-context.jsx)
- [grievanceController.js](backend/src/controllers/grievanceController.js)
- [VoiceInput.jsx](Frontend/src/components/VoiceInput.jsx)
- [SubmitComplaint.jsx](Frontend/src/pages/SubmitComplaint.jsx)
- [Grievance.js](backend/src/models/Grievance.js)

</blog>
